title: 10 best herbal teas for swollen gums

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great burnet teas helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers contain powerful compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums. When brewed into a tea, these compounds are released and can be absorbed by the body, helping to soothe and calm inflamed gum tissue. Additionally, great burnet teas may also help to reduce inflammation-causing bacteria in the mouth, further contributing to its beneficial effects on swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Great Burnet tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling by reducing their ability to multiply and cause infection.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.

Antioxidant activity: Great Burnet tea is rich in antioxidants, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which can help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots are effective in reducing swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: Containing tannins, the barks help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried sanguisorba officinalis roots and flowers measuring one teaspoon of each per cup of boiling water.
Combine the ingredients in a heat-resistant glass container or teapot with 200-250 ml of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids retaining the clear liquid.
Cool the tea to room temperature before consuming it several times a day for optimal relief from swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain. The tea's antioxidants also help to combat the bacteria that cause gum inflammation, promoting a healthy balance in the mouth. Additionally, broad-leaved lime teas contain flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of plaque and tartar, further contributing to a reduction in swollen gums and overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: The tea's antibacterial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation and swelling, reducing the risk of periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory agents, including polyphenols and terpenes, reduce swelling and pain in gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antioxidant properties: The tea's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect gum tissue from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: The barks of the broad-leaved lime plant have antibacterial properties that help combat infections that can cause gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh broad-leaved lime leaves in the early morning or evening when they are most aromatic.
Rinse the collected leaves thoroughly under cold running water and pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Combine the rinsed leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass teapot, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding any solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, taking up to three cups per day as needed for relief from swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with swollen gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids, which work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tea's antioxidant properties also help to soothe and calm irritated gum tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of pot marigold tea can help to combat underlying bacterial infections that may be contributing to swollen gums, providing natural relief from this common oral health issue.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are pro-inflammatory hormones.
Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea's antioxidant properties help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate swollen gums and promote healing.

Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain a rich source of antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, promoting healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula flowers in the morning after dew has dried, ensuring no pesticides or contaminants are present.
Rinse the freshly harvested calendula flowers gently with cool filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one tablespoon of dried calendula petals or one teaspoon of fresh petals with one cup of boiling water in a medium-sized heat-resistant glass bowl.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal goodness to infuse into the hot water and release its anti-inflammatory properties.
Strain the tea into a large ceramic cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving the tea warm three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain. The gel-like substance found in aloe vera contains powerful compounds that inhibit prostaglandins, which are responsible for inflammation in the body. When consumed as a tea, these compounds work to soothe and calm irritated gum tissues, providing fast relief from swollen gums. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and fungi, promoting healthy gum growth and preventing further irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, making it an effective natural remedy for gum inflammation.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants in aloe vera tea help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate gum inflammation and damage, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and reducing oxidative stress.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and promote cell proliferation accelerates the healing process of swollen gums, helping to reduce pain, discomfort, and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves contain a compound called acemannan, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one cup of dried aloe vera leaves gently under cold running water for ten seconds.
Chop the rinsed aloe vera leaves into small pieces weighing approximately two teaspoons.
Boil one quart of filtered water in a saucepan for eight minutes and let it cool slightly.
Steep the chopped aloe vera pieces in boiling water for seventeen to twenty minutes or until desired strength.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The menthol in peppermint also has a numbing effect on the gum tissue, providing temporary relief from pain and sensitivity. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties can help combat gum disease-causing bacteria, promoting overall oral health. By drinking herbal peppermint tea regularly, individuals can experience a soothing and calming effect on their gums, helping to alleviate swollen gums and promote healthy, happy teeth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint tea's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacteria and fungi that can cause gum inflammation and infection, reducing swelling and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The menthol content in peppermint tea has a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which helps reduce pain, swelling, and redness associated with swollen gums.
Antioxidant properties: Peppermint tea's antioxidants help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain compounds like menthol and menthone, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce bleeding and inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves from your garden or store-bought dried peppermint leaves in an airtight container.
Measure out two tablespoons of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured dried peppermint leaves with the boiling water in a large glass teapot and allow it to steep for seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before gargling with one tablespoon of the warm peppermint tea two times daily for optimal benefits.

remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves in two cups of water for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot discard solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey and stir well to combine sweeten to taste.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional three minutes before serving hot.
Drink the tea slowly and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation while warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sage leaves, measuring one teaspoon of dried leaves per eight ounces water.

The tea's high concentration of allantoin, a natural compound, promotes healing and regenerates new cells, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, comfrey's antibacterial properties combat gum disease-causing bacteria, promoting healthy gums and reducing symptoms such as pain and bleeding.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation, allowing for a faster recovery.
Saponins: Saponins have antibacterial properties, which combat the underlying bacterial infection causing swollen gums, promoting healthy gum tissue growth and reducing inflammation.

Vitamins and Minerals: Comfrey tea is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which support overall oral health by nourishing and strengthening the gums and bones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in gum tissue.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem (rhizome) of comfrey is rich in mucilage, a soothing substance that can help ease gum discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves of symphytum officinale in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried herbs or two teaspoons of fresh herbs per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before drinking.
Consume the tea warm or at room temperature, ideally three times a day for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The isoflavones present in red clover, specifically formononetin and biochanin A, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate gum swelling by blocking the production of enzymes that contribute to inflammatory responses. Additionally, red clover's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can exacerbate gum inflammation, promoting a healthier oral environment and reducing discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover tea contains isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for gingivitis.

Antibacterial properties: The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibit antibacterial activity against certain bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans, helping to prevent further inflammation and damage to the gums.

Antioxidant activity: Red clover tea is rich in antioxidants like quercetin and kaempferol, which can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their flavonoid content, which has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, helping to combat infection and promote healing in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh trifolium pratense flowers or use dried flowers in amounts of 1-2 tablespoons.
Combine the flowers with boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste and store the tea in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.
Consume the tea twice daily, one hour before meals, for a duration of 7-10 days.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen gums with herbal teas is by using peppermint tea, chamomile tea, and sage tea. These teas have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. To prepare the tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain and rinse your mouth with the cooled tea several times a day to experience relief from swollen gums.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal teas are various inflammatory conditions, including mouth sores, canker sores, and periodontitis. Teas like chamomile, peppermint, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Other herbs like sage, eucalyptus, and licorice root also provide relief from gum inflammation and sensitivity. These teas can be consumed hot or cold, depending on individual preferences.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for swollen gums are a natural remedy that provides relief from inflammation and discomfort in the gums. These teas are made from herbs such as peppermint, chamomile, and calendula, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to reduce swelling and promote healing. Drinking these teas can help to soothe sore gums and improve oral health, allowing individuals to eat and smile with confidence again.
